text: in this video we will reveal our top
seven Candlestick patterns going from
Basics to Advanced patterns
we will also show you how to use these
patterns in combination with price
action to spot high probability trade
opportunities all in all this is going
to be a Candlestick Trading masterclass
a quick note before we start if you are
interested in our free training click
the link in the description to know more
it's free and with that being said let's
get started
what are candlesticks
candlesticks are just a simple way of
looking at the historical price of the
asset
each Candlestick represents the price
movement of the asset for a particular
period of time so if we are on a daily
chart a single candle represents the
price action for one day
on the chart we see two types of candles
green candles and red candles
[Music]
a green candle is formed when the
closing price is higher than the opening
price
this indicates that the buyers were
stronger than the sellers
similarly a red candle is formed when
the closing price is lower than the
opening price
it indicates that the sellers were
stronger than the buyers
now the reason why most Traders use
candlesticks is because it gives us a
better understanding of the price
movement
a Candlestick consists of two parts the
Wicks or shadows and the body
the end of the upper Wick shows us the
highest price achieved during that time
similarly the end of the lower Wick
shows us the lowest price achieved
during that period
when it comes to the candle body things
start to differ
for green candles the lower end of the
body is the opening price and the upper
end is the closing price
on the red candle the upper end of the
body is the open and the lower end is
the close
before we move on to the Candlestick
patterns we need to understand a few
Concepts about candlesticks
concept one
Big versus small body
the body of a particular candle can tell
us a lot about the strength or
weaknesses of the buyers and sellers
for example here we have two green
candles the candle on the left has a big
body and relatively small wicks
the price opened down here
initially the sellers tried to push the
price down the sellers were very weak as
the buyers quickly came in and took the
price higher
then the buyers were able to travel a
lot of distance without any pressure
from the sellers
as a result the price closed very near
to the high
this shows us that the buyers were
significantly stronger than the sellers
during this candle
on the second candle the story is
different
the price opened here and the buyers
started to move the price
they raised the price to a new high but
at the high the sellers stepped in and
took the price downwards
the sellers were powerful and nearly
covered the entire distance back to the
opening price
but the buyers just about managed to
close the price above the open
in this instance we can see that the
buyers were stronger than the sellers
but the difference between their power
was insignificant
when the candle's body is relatively
bigger than the Wicks it shows that
either the buyers or the sellers were in
control
on the other hand when the body is
smaller than the Wicks it shows that
there was much less control from either
side it was a tough contest between
buyers and sellers
concept 2 large wicks
whenever we spot a large Wick above or
below the body it gives us a strong clue
about the price action
if we spot a large Wick above the body
it shows us that the sellers are
strongly selling on these levels for the
price to move higher the buyers will
need a lot of effort
similarly if we spot a large Wick below
the body it shows us that the buyers are
strongly buying at these lower levels
If the sellers want to move the price
lower they must overcome these buyers
so the concepts of body and Wicks are
essential moving forward keep them in
mind as we begin to understand some
basic Candlestick patterns if you are
enjoying this video so far then be sure
to hit the like button and subscribe to
our channel so that you never miss any
of our new videos
basic patterns
pattern number one bullish pin bar
a bullish pin bar is a strong reversal
Candlestick pattern it indicates a
potential reversal to the upside
a bullish pin bar consists of three
elements a small green body a large Wick
below the body a tiny or no Wick above
the body
let's understand the psychology behind
this pattern
a bullish pin bar shows that the price
opened around this area and instantly
the sellers tried to take the prices
down
they were able to push the prices down
but at some point the lower prices
triggered buy orders from the buyers
the buyers were significantly stronger
than the sellers as they pushed the
price beyond the open therefore if we
think about it the buyers covered more
distance than the sellers
it indicates that the buyers have
stepped in and they are trying to take
the price higher
if a pin bar appears after a strong down
move it indicates that the sellers have
lost their strength and the buyers are
now stepping in to move the price
upwards
to trade the pin bar we must look at
other confluences to confirm our bias we
will discuss that later on but if we
find a buying opportunity as a pin bar
we can place a buy order above the high
of the pin bar we can set our stop loss
below the pin bar and Target the next
resistance
pattern number two
bearish pin bar
the bearish pin bar is just the opposite
of the bullish pin bar it is a red
candle with a small body a large Wick
above the body and a tiny or no Wick
below the body
it indicates a reversal to the downside
the psychology behind the pattern is
very simple
the price opened here and the buyers
started to move the markets upwards they
covered some distance and created this
High the sellers then get into the mood
and push the prices downwards Crossing
below the opening price
the sellers then managed to keep the
price down and the candle closes near
its lows
this indicates that the sellers have
momentarily gained an advantage over the
buyers
if we find another Confluence at this
point then we can place a sell trade
below the low of the pin bar and we can
keep our stop loss just above the pin
bar
three doji
the doji pattern is probably the only
Candlestick pattern that does not give
us directional bias however it is
essential to understand this pattern
a doji candle is characterized by a
candle with approximately equal Wicks
above and below a small body
the open and close are somewhere in the
middle of the candle and the body of the
candle is very small
it shows that the buyers and sellers
were very competitive during that time
period the sellers tried to push the
prices down while the buyers push the
prices upwards
in the end they were both equal to each
other and the price closed near the open
in itself the doji tells us nothing it
can indicate a momentary pause in the
move but if we see a few doji in a row
the buyers and sellers are battling for
a win usually after such a pattern the
price will generally break out a wild
move in either direction so keep that in
mind
the three patterns covered up until this
point are a single candle pattern
now we will move to intermediate
patterns that consist of two candles
pattern number four bullish engulfing
bar
a bullish engulfing bar is a two candle
pattern where a green candle completely
engulfs a red candle before it
this means we first see a red candle
then the next candle is a big green
candle that completely engulfs the red
candle
the high of the green candle is higher
than the high of the red candle
similarly the low of the green candle is
lower than the low of the red candle
the psychology behind this pattern is
pretty simple the sellers were in
control during this red candle then as
the new candle opened the sellers took
the price lower it broke the previous
low and suddenly the buyers stepped in
they overwhelmed the sellers and brought
the price beyond the last candle's High
it shows that the buyers covered a more
significant distance than the sellers
this means the buyers were now in
control and ready to take the price
higher
for the entry we enter at the break of
the high we can place our stop loss
below the low of the candle
again as I already said we want to see
other confluences like the price action
to confirm a trade
pattern number five bearish engulfing
bar
a bearish engulfing bar is a two candle
formation where a big red candle
completely engulfs the previous green
candle we first have a green candle then
the next candle is a larger red candle
that completely engulfs the green candle
the low of the red candle is lower than
the previous candles low
similarly the high is higher than the
previous candles High
let's understand the psychology of the
pattern
first we see that the buyers were in
control as the price made this green
candle then as the next candle opens the
buyers try to push the price upwards
they managed to break above the high of
the previous candle then all of a sudden
sellers see these highs as an
opportunity and they start to sell
their selling pressure is so high that
they managed to break beyond the low of
the previous candle
the sellers traveled a lot of distance
compared to the buyers hence the sellers
have now grabbed control of the markets
so we can place a sell trade below the
low of this candle and we can set our
stop loss above the high
Advanced patterns
pattern number six Morning Star pattern
the Morning Star pattern is a three
candle pattern that indicates a bullish
price reversal
the first candle is a strong red candle
with a big enough body
the second candle is a doji or pin bar
candle with a small body and large wicks
the doji or the pin bar can be red or
green
then finally we have a big green candle
that breaks above the high of the Doshi
candle
the sides of this candle will determine
the strength of the pattern if the
candle is as big as the red candle then
it is a good trade opportunity
if it is smaller than the red candle it
is a low probability trade
lastly if the green candle closes above
the high of the red candle it is a
strong reversal candle
the psychology behind the pattern is
pretty simple first we have a red candle
this shows us that the sellers were in
complete control of the markets they
pushed the prices downwards
then we see a small doji candle it shows
us that buyers have stepped in and are
pushing the prices upwards
soon the sellers lose their strength and
the buyers take the prices higher
creating this green candle
it shows that the buyers have turned
around the situation and are now likely
to see an up move
to enter the trade we will place a buy
order above the high of the green candle
we will keep our stop loss below the
doji candle
pattern number seven Evening Star
pattern
the evening star pattern is the complete
opposite of the Morning Star pattern
here we have a three candle pattern
where the first candle is a green candle
with a considerably big body
then we have a small doji candle with a
very small body and finally we have a
red candle with a big body
the psychology behind this pattern shows
us that initially the buyers were in
control as they created this green
candle then soon enough the sellers
entered and started to challenge the
buyers as a result we see this doji
candle the doji candle indicates that
the buyers and sellers are in a tug of
war
then finally the sellers win the battle
and they start to move the price down
to enter a sell trade here we place a
sell trade below the low of this candle
and keep our stop loss above the doji
candle
so these were the seven Candlestick
patterns that can help you spot trade
opportunities but we are not done yet we
will now understand how to use price
action Concepts to find entries using
these patterns
we will use support and resistance
levels and trend lines to combine the
Candlestick patterns with price action
here is an example
the price was in a downtrend but it
found support near this area we see two
reversals here and Mark it as a support
level
then the third time when the price
arrives near the support we expect it to
find support but we are waiting for a
Candlestick confirmation
on the down move here we see that as the
price approached the support the candles
became smaller in size this was the
first sign that the sellers were losing
momentum
then we see this doji candle form at the
support another sign of support
the doji is followed by a large green
candle indicating that the buyers had
taken over the control and were ready to
move the price
the green candle completed a Morning
Star pattern it was a strong pattern
because the green candle broke above the
high of the doji candle and the red
candle before it
so a Buy trade can be placed above the
high of the green candle
we can place our stop loss below the pin
bar and we can Target this high
as you can see the price moved up after
our entry
even if you missed the entry the price
also gave us a second entry
the price made these two tops creating a
solid trend line
when the price reaches near the trend
line again we wait to see if the price
develops some pattern
this red candle indicates that the
sellers were trying to provide
resistance at the trend line
the candle is followed by a large green
candle that completely engulfs the red
candle it's a bullish engulfing candle
the low of the green candle is lower
than the red candle the high is higher
than the red candle
again a very strong indication that the
buyers have taken control and the trend
line is not strong anymore
a Buy trade could be initiated here as
well
we place a buy order above the high of
the candle and we put a stop loss below
the candle
our targets will be these highs
as you can see the price achieved our
Targets on this trade
here is an example of a cell trade
here we see that the price finds
resistance around this area then when
the price tests this resistance the
second time we see it create an evening
star pattern
we have a green candle a doji followed
by a large red candle the red candle is
so large that it closes Way Beyond the
lows of the previous two candles hence
it is a high probability pattern
so we place a sell trade at this
candle's low and set our stop loss above
the high
for our targets we can Target these two
lows
and as you can see the price achieved
our targets
I hope you have now understood how to
use these candlesticks for your trading
again the candlesticks alone are nothing
you need to combine them with other
factors to create a trading system
